purpose: Keep capability discovery broad enough to preserve MCP, tools, runtime adapters, memory, graph, dependency, and worker-task capabilities as first-class route inputs, while avoiding noisy default expansion into every concrete subtool when no route, risk, authorization, or verification decision depends on that detail.
corePrinciple: Fetch records first-class capability families first; Thinking expands only the families that materially affect owner, weapon, runtime, permission, risk, writeback, or verification.

globalProfessionalProviderFirst.principle: Prefer existing professional global or project providers before creating or upgrading an execution agent.
globalProfessionalProviderFirst.workerTaskBoundary: worker_task is a run-scoped dispatch packet for a selected owner/loadout; it is not a temporary agent identity, subagent definition, or durable provider.
globalProfessionalProviderFirst.creationGate: create_agent is allowed only when global and project professional providers have been checked and cannot own the recurring responsibility class.

expandWhen: authorization, credential, paid action, or external write risk exists
runtime or OS support changes the route
verification depends on a concrete provider/tool
a concrete subtool is needed for rollback, audit, or public-ready evidence
doNotExpandWhen: the family is only recorded to preserve first-class status
no owner, weapon, permission, risk, or verification decision changes
expansion would dump unrelated subtools into user-facing output

acceptanceCriteria.providerTypeCoverage: All runtimeProviderTypesRequiredInDefaultArtifact appear in the default governed execution artifact.
acceptanceCriteria.workerTaskCoverage: Thinking produces workerTaskPackets separately from capabilityInventory so task dispatch is not collapsed into provider discovery.
acceptanceCriteria.searchLogCoverage: Fetch capabilityDiscovery.searchLog records checked source families, including empty or unavailable sources.
acceptanceCriteria.professionalProviderFirst: Before create_agent or upgrade_existing_owner, the route records checked global/project professional providers across agent, skill, command_script, MCP, runtime_tool, plugin_connector, dependency_provider, memory_graph, hook_runtime_adapter, and prompt_rule_workflow families.
acceptanceCriteria.workerTaskIdentityBoundary: workerTaskPackets may carry concrete run work, but they must not be counted as execution_agent providers or durable identities.
acceptanceCriteria.noExpansionNeededPolicy: A safe route may record no_expansion_needed for concrete subtools, but not for the first-class capability family itself.
